input.command: git ls-files | head -50 && echo "---" && cat package.json

content.text:
Root cause found. In `src/paginate.js:5`, the slice is `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)` — that `- 1` returns `size - 1` items (9 instead of 10), while the label in `summary.js` correctly uses the full `size`. That mismatch is exactly "showing 1-10 but 9 rows."

Let me add a failing test that reproduces it, then fix.

content.text: Failing test confirms the root cause. Now the single fix:

input.old_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
input.new_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

content.text:
Fixed. All 4 tests pass.

**Root cause:** `src/paginate.js:5` sliced with `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)`. `Array.slice(start, end)` treats `end` as exclusive, so subtracting 1 returned only `size - 1` rows (9 for a page size of 10). Meanwhile `rangeLabel` in `src/summary.js` correctly used the full `size` for its "1-10" label — hence "showing 1-10 of 23" next to only 9 rows.

**Fix:** one character — dropped the `- 1` so the slice end is `start + size`, yielding a full page and letting the short final page fall out naturally (page 3 of 23 → rows 21-23).

I added three `paginate` tests first (full page, row-count-matches-label, short final page) to reproduce the bug before touching the code.

result:
Fixed. All 4 tests pass.

**Root cause:** `src/paginate.js:5` sliced with `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)`. `Array.slice(start, end)` treats `end` as exclusive, so subtracting 1 returned only `size - 1` rows (9 for a page size of 10). Meanwhile `rangeLabel` in `src/summary.js` correctly used the full `size` for its "1-10" label — hence "showing 1-10 of 23" next to only 9 rows.

**Fix:** one character — dropped the `- 1` so the slice end is `start + size`, yielding a full page and letting the short final page fall out naturally (page 3 of 23 → rows 21-23).

I added three `paginate` tests first (full page, row-count-matches-label, short final page) to reproduce the bug before touching the code.
